title: Get Step Type Schema Response
description: Response format for the get_step_type_schema MCP tool. Returns complete metadata and configuration schema for a specific step type.

typeId.description: Unique identifier matching Kettle's internal step name

category.description: Functional category of the step type

description.description: Action-oriented description of step purpose and common use cases

default.description: Default value if field is optional

min.description: Minimum value for numbers or minimum length for strings

max.description: Maximum value for numbers or maximum length for strings

nested.description: Nested fields for object or array types (recursive structure)

examples.description: Example configurations demonstrating common usage patterns

name.description: Short, descriptive name for the scenario

description.description: Detailed explanation of what this configuration does and when to use it

configuration.description: Complete configuration object matching the step type's schema

examples.description: Read data from a database table using SQL queries. Supports parameterized queries, connection pooling, and result set streaming for efficient data extraction.

fields.name: connection
fields.description: Database connection name (must be defined in Kettle's connection repository)

fields.name: sql
fields.description: SQL query to execute. Supports parameter substitution using ? placeholders.

fields.name: limit
fields.description: Maximum number of rows to return (0 = no limit)

fields.name: enableLazyConversion
fields.description: Optimize memory usage by deferring type conversion until field access

examples.name: Read Customer Orders
examples.description: Read all customer orders from MySQL orders table with timestamp filtering for incremental loads

configuration.sql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE created_at > ? ORDER BY created_at

examples.name: Read Top Products
examples.description: Read top 100 best-selling products from PostgreSQL with aggregated sales data

configuration.sql: SELECT product_id, product_name, SUM(quantity) as total_sales FROM sales GROUP BY product_id, product_name ORDER BY total_sales DESC
